tokentop includes 15 carefully crafted themes. Each is designed to look great in the terminal and provide clear visual hierarchy for usage data.

## Dark themes

### Tokyo Night

The default theme. Cool blues and purples inspired by the Tokyo Night color scheme. Excellent contrast for extended monitoring sessions.

```
ttop demo --theme tokyo-night
```

<Image src={tokyoNight} alt="Tokyo Night theme preview" />

### Dracula

The popular Dracula palette with its signature purple, pink, and cyan accents on a dark background.

```
ttop demo --theme dracula
```

<Image src={dracula} alt="Dracula theme preview" />

### Nord

Arctic, north-bluish color palette with muted tones. Clean and easy on the eyes.

```
ttop demo --theme nord
```

<Image src={nord} alt="Nord theme preview" />

### One Dark

Based on Atom's One Dark theme. Warm accent colors on a neutral dark background.

```
ttop demo --theme one-dark
```

<Image src={oneDark} alt="One Dark theme preview" />

### Catppuccin Mocha

The darkest flavor of the Catppuccin palette. Rich, warm colors with a soothing dark base.

```
ttop demo --theme catppuccin-mocha
```

<Image src={catppuccinMocha} alt="Catppuccin Mocha theme preview" />

### Gruvbox Dark

Retro groove colors with warm, earthy tones. High contrast yellow, green, and orange accents.

```
ttop demo --theme gruvbox-dark
```

<Image src={gruvboxDark} alt="Gruvbox Dark theme preview" />

### Kanagawa

Inspired by the famous wave painting. Deep blues with warm accent colors from the Japanese palette.

```
ttop demo --theme kanagawa
```

<Image src={kanagawa} alt="Kanagawa theme preview" />

### Rosé Pine

Soho vibes with muted pinks, golds, and greens on a very dark base.

```
ttop demo --theme rose-pine
```

<Image src={rosePine} alt="Rosé Pine theme preview" />

### OpenCode

A theme designed to match the OpenCode terminal interface. Dark background with blue accents.

```
ttop demo --theme opencode
```

<Image src={opencode} alt="OpenCode theme preview" />

### Claude Code

Matches the Claude Code terminal aesthetic. Dark with warm amber and orange accents.

```
ttop demo --theme claude-code
```

<Image src={claudeCode} alt="Claude Code theme preview" />

## Light themes

### GitHub Light

Clean and minimal, inspired by GitHub's light UI. High contrast black text on a white-adjacent background.

```
ttop demo --theme github-light
```

<Image src={githubLight} alt="GitHub Light theme preview" />

### Catppuccin Latte

The lightest Catppuccin flavor. Pastel accents on a warm light background.

```
ttop demo --theme catppuccin-latte
```

<Image src={catppuccinLatte} alt="Catppuccin Latte theme preview" />

### Gruvbox Light

The light variant of Gruvbox. Warm, paper-like background with earthy accent colors.

```
ttop demo --theme gruvbox-light
```

<Image src={gruvboxLight} alt="Gruvbox Light theme preview" />

### Rosé Pine Dawn

The light variant of Rosé Pine. Soft pinks and golds on a warm light base.

```
ttop demo --theme rose-pine-dawn
```

<Image src={rosePineDawn} alt="Rosé Pine Dawn theme preview" />

### Solarized Light

Ethan Schoonover's precision-engineered color scheme. Carefully balanced contrast with distinctive yellow and blue accents.

```
ttop demo --theme solarized-light
```

<Image src={solarizedLight} alt="Solarized Light theme preview" />

## Previewing themes

The fastest way to preview any theme is by passing `--theme` to demo mode:

```bash
ttop demo --theme dracula
```

You can also change themes at runtime: press `,` (comma) to open Settings, navigate to Display, and cycle through available themes. Or use the command palette (`:`) and search for "Theme:".
